item.title: Determinants
item.description: This topic is sometimes not covered in precalculus classes. It is more often included as a part of a linear algebra class covered in college.

item.title: Equations of Lines
item.description: Various equations can form a line in the xy plane when graphed.

item.title: Expectation Values
item.description: In probability, we often have events that have a variety of possible numerical outcomes.

item.title: Arithmetic Sequences
item.description: An arithmetic sequence is a sequence of numbers such that the difference between every term and the term that came before it is the same.

item.title: Dot Products
item.description: Definition in Terms of Vector Magnitudes and Angles The dot product \vec{\mathbf a} \cdot \vec{\mathbf b} of two vectors \vec{\mathbf a} and \vec{\mathbf b} is defined in terms of the vector magnitudes a and b as well as the angle between the vectors \theta.

item.title: Factorials
item.description: Definition for Factorials of Natural Numbers A factorial of some natural number n, written as n! and read as ”n factorial,” is the product of all natural numbers less than or equal to n.

item.title: Factoring Patterns
item.description: Below is a reference page containing various useful factoring patterns for real numbers.

item.title: Welcome to Trevor's Notes
item.description: What is Trevor’s Notes? My name is Trevor Kafka, I am a math and physics tutor, and Trevor’s Notes is a project that began in July 2025.

item.title: Volume
item.description: Prisms The volume of a prism can be found by multiplying the base area of the prism A by the height of the prism h.

item.title: TI Calculator Reference
item.description: On this page I will provide some general guidance on how to use the TI 83–84 calculators’ most important functionalities.
